stimulus: Mahasweta Devi's novel Hajar Churashir Maa (1974) explores the political upheavals of late 1960s West Bengal through the grief of a mother whose son has been killed. With its unflinching social criticism, polyphonic narration, and dexterous incorporation of Bengali oral storytelling, the novel galvanized a generation of South Asian writers. ______blank those who took up its style and concerns are often grouped together as the Mahasweta School novelists.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. "Fittingly" is a transition that means "appropriately" or "suitably," and it is appropriate that novelists who took up Mahasweta Devi's style and concerns would come to be known as the Mahasweta School novelists.

Choice A is incorrect because "instead" uses a disagreement transition, but this sentence is not disagreeing with anything—rather, it is explaining the fitting label given to writers who adopted her style. Choice B is incorrect because "by comparison" signals a comparison between two cases, when this sentence is not comparing the novelists' style to Mahasweta Devi's. Choice D is incorrect because "nevertheless" signals contrast, when this sentence in fact describes an apt consequence of the novel's influence.
